acceptance_criteria: A Rust validator enforces stage presence, handoffs, profile evidence, parent/child links, and changed-path coverage.
CI runs the validator separately from the Rust workspace quality gate.

open_questions.question: Changed-path coverage ignores work-items/ because those artifacts are process metadata rather than target implementation paths.

objective: Plan the validator, fixtures, and CI wiring.
change_slices: Add the process-method crate and CLI.
Add valid fixtures plus failing mutation tests.
Add a dedicated GitHub Actions workflow and PR template.
test_scenarios: Cross-cutting and single-module fixtures validate successfully.
Missing stages, missing handoffs, missing module evidence, and uncovered changed paths fail validation.

notes: The validator is implemented once in shared tooling and reused by CI and local execution.
The validator enforces process completeness and does not embed business policy logic.

rollout_notes: Run the dedicated process workflow alongside the Rust workspace workflow.
